title: Podman 5.0.0 fails to build image on rootful machine
body:
### Issue Description

I updated podman 5.0.0 today and tried to build an image on macOS(Arm64) Host, but it failed with an error message related to the pasta.

### Steps to reproduce the issue


Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a simple Dockerfile like below:
```Dockerfile
FROM fedora:39

RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/share/ca-certificates
```

2. Run `podman build -t test .`

### Describe the results you received

The build fails with the following error:
```
$ podman build -t test .
STEP 1/2: FROM fedora:39
STEP 2/2: RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/share/ca-certificates
error running container: did not get container start message from parent: EOF
Error: building at STEP "RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/share/ca-certificates": setup network: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Don't run as root. Changing to nobody...
No routable interface for IPv6: IPv6 is disabled
Couldn't open network namespace /proc/2381/ns/net: Permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

The build should succeed.

### podman info output

podman version 5.0.0
$ podman info

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details


Host Information
```
$ sw_vers -productVersion
14.4
$ uname -m
arm64
```
Above command worked on macOS(x86_64) Host.

Fedora Core information
```
# cat /etc/*-release | grep PRETTY_NAME
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora CoreOS 39.20240309.2.0"
# pasta --version | head -1
pasta 0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc39.aarch64-pasta
```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: @bc-lee, thanks for reporting this! Could you please have a look at SELinux denials (`/var/log/audit/audit.log` or using a tool of your choice)?

Is `container-selinux` installed in the image? If it is not installed try adding it with `rpm-ostree install container-selinux` then reboot.
If it is installed please check the selinux label with `ls -Z /usr/bin/podman` it should be `system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0`

> Could you please have a look at SELinux denials (/var/log/audit/audit.log or using a tool of your choice)?

Well, it seems that no SELinux denials are present in the audit log. I ran `# grep -i "denied" /var/log/audit/audit.log` and got no output.

I don't think this is related to SELinux.
```
# rpm-ostree install container-selinux
error: "container-selinux" is already provided by: container-selinux-2:2.229.0-1.fc39.noarch. Use --allow-inactive to explicitly require it.
# ls -Z /usr/bin/podman
system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0 /usr/bin/podman
# uname -a
Linux localhost.localdomain 6.7.7-200.fc39.aarch64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Mar  1 17:20:07 UTC 2024 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

Maybe this is related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22052 ?

> Maybe this is related to #22052 ?

I don't think it has anything to do with that, actually. One thing that strikes me here is that pasta(1) is started as root in this case, and we don't quite expect that. I'm not sure if this is the intended behaviour for podman machine. If pasta is started as root, we might end up dropping additional capabilities compared to a regular user.

Wait a minute? You are running podman as root, that should never use pasta there. Pasta will not run as root:
> Don't run as root. Changing to nobody...

Therefore it can never open the netns which is expected
Does `podman run` work?

> Wait a minute? You are running podman as root

But the `$` in `$ podman build -t test .` doesn't look like root...? I'm confused.

> But the `$` in `$ podman build -t test .` doesn't look like root...? I'm confused.

Yes, `$ podman run` works and can perform network operations.

I'm running on a non-root macOS account, where the podman machine is created with the `--rootful` option.
Yeah I think this is once again a common podman-remote bug where we check privs on the remote side rather than on the server.

Ok so yeah that is trivial to reproduce even on linux by running the service as root then connecting as rootless.
And I knew this was familiar to me, as I fixed the inverse problem (root->rootless) a while ago https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/dbfe79757b33231b3e5dc8537deb85fba8402eef.

I guess nobody noticed the problem so far because slirp4netns can be run as root so it never failed until we switched the default to pasta. so I guess a workaround is to use `podman build --network slirp4netns ...` or if do not not need any networking then `--network none`
I think they might be seeing something like this with podman desktop now.

Can I reopen this issue? Unfortunately, [the proposed fix](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22122) does not work for me. I only checked the `podman-remote` side. If the isolation is set to `IsolationOCIRootless`, the `podman-remote` command fails, just as I initially reported. However, if I modify the code or set a command-line flag like `--isolation=default` or `--isolation=oci` to use `IsolationDefault` or `IsolationOCI`, then `podman build` works as expected. Note that, `unshare.IsRootless()` always returns `true` on macOS. [Link](https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/6c835719e98e403dae4a437e3809e247c9561b29/pkg/unshare/unshare_darwin.go)
the fix only worked on the server so you need to update the server not the client, the client was fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22133
As of e852b970f609b62202e212fad8de63cadd1d3d29, my issue is fixed. Thanks for the quick response!

I am seeing a similar issue with `podman run` after upgrading our podman in container setup to podman v5. It uses a rootful remote podman socket. 

```
podman run hello-world
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 81df7ff16254 done   |
Copying config 5dd467fce5 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: setting up Pasta: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Don't run as root. Changing to nobody...
```

Running it with `--network slirp4netns` works.

> I am seeing a similar issue with `podman run` after upgrading our podman in container setup to podman v5. It uses a rootful remote podman socket.

What Podman version are you using, exactly? This is fixed only starting from 5.0.1.

> > I am seeing a similar issue with `podman run` after upgrading our podman in container setup to podman v5. It uses a rootful remote podman socket.
> 
> What Podman version are you using, exactly? This is fixed only starting from 5.0.1.

podman v5.0.3

@brianmcarey please file a new issue and provide a full reproducer there.
This here was a very specific issue about podman build so it cannot be related to yours.

> @brianmcarey please file a new issue and provide a full reproducer there. This here was a very specific issue about podman build so it cannot be related to yours.

I've opened an issue here - https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22907 - thanks @Luap99
